titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Printmakers Gathering Portfolio
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 03:28

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Inkjet prints created in 2011 by printmakers of their own black-and-white photographic work, 1995-2011, that they compiled as portfolios and exchanged in conjunction with the meeting of the Society of Photographic Educators in Atlanta, Georgia, March 2011.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Printmakers Gathering Portfolio. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Each folder in the collection contains a single inkjet print.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints created in 2011 by printmakers of their own black-and-white photographic work, 1995-2011, that they compiled as portfolios and exchanged in conjunction with the meeting of the Society of Photographic Educators in Atlanta, Georgia, March 2011. Individuals with work represented in the portfolio include Walker Blackwell, Tyler Boley, Jon Cone, John Dean, Lauren Henkin, Bill Kennedy, J. Scott King, and Mark Savoia. The guide to the collection includes descriptions of image capture techniques, scanner technologies, and printer methods. Cone summarized some of the printing methods in his "The State of the State of the Arts in Black & White" in View Camera Magazine (January/February 2012).

accessrestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
accessrestrict.p: The Printmakers Gathering Portfolio is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Walker Blackwell is a printmaker, educator, and founder of Black Point Editions in Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From a body of work, Shadow Portraits .

Photographic image created with a Hasselblad 500 C camera and 60-millimeter lens on Kodak T-MAX 100 film, scanned using Scanview ScanMate 11000 scanner, and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 4000 using ErgoSoft Studio Print Raster Image Processing Software on Canson Infinity Rag Photographique 210 grams per square meter matte paper using PiezoTone Selenium K7 ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From a body of work, Cement .

Photographic image created with a Hasselblad 500 C camera and 60-millimeter lens on Kodak T-MAX 100 film, scanned using Scanview ScanMate 11000 scanner, and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9900 using ColorBurst Raster Image Processing software on Epson Exhibition Fiber Paper using Epson UltraChrome High Dynamic Range ink and Cone Editions Gloss Optimizer.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tyler Boley is a photographer, printmaker, educator, and founder of Custom Digital LLC.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From a series of eight images, Elizabeth G .
Printed on Arches Natural White Cold Press Watercolor Paper using Epson UltraChrome High Dynamic Range ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From an ongoing body of work of Pacific Northwest landscapes.

Printed on Canson Infinity Edition Etching Rag 210 grams per square meter paper using a custom ink blend of Cone PiezoTone Warm Neutral, Cone PiezoTone Carbon Sepia, Cone PiezoTone Selenium Tone, and Cone Piezography Sepia K7 ink.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jon Cone is a artist, printmaker, developer of technology, and educator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alternate title on verso, My trees off the old county road .
Custom monochromatic process on handmade paper.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Dean is a photographer, printmaker, and founder of Dean Imaging.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From series Buildings in Motion .

Photographic image created with a Horseman 450 camera and a 90-millimeter Rodenstock Grandagon lens on Ilford HPS film, scanned using a Howtek drum scanner and printed by a Hewlett Packard DesignJet 23100 printer using Qimage Ultimate software on Canson Infinity Rag Photographique 210 grams per square meter matte paper using Hewlett Packard Vivera ink and coated with Moab Desert Varnish Lacquer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From series Buildings in Motion .
Print made with Split QuadTone Raster Image Processing software using Cone Piezography Warm Neutral K7 and Cone Piezography Selenium K7 ink

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From series Before and After Pangaea that looks at isolated geological formations in the Appalachian Mountains.

Photographic image created with a Wista 45 camera and a 90-millimeter Rodenstock Grandagon lens on Kodak T-MAX 100 film, scanned using a Howtek drum scanner and printed by a Hewlett Packard DesignJet Z3100 Photo Printer using Qimage Ultimate software on Cone 55 natural gloss fiber rag paper and coated with Moab Desert Varnish Lacquer.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lauren Henkin is a photographer and sculptor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From series Displaced Part I , image created in Nova Scotia, Canada.
Photographic image created with a Wista 45 camera, printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 4800 printer using QuadTone Raster Image Processing Software on Hahnemühle William Turner Inkjet Paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From series Silence is an Orchard .
Image created in Acadia National Park.
Photographic image created with a Wista 45 camera, printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 4800 printer using QuadTone Raster Image Processing Software on kozo paper.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bill Kennedy is a photographer and printmaker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image created in Louisiana.

Photographic image created with a Wista 45 camera and a 135-millimeter Fujinon lens on Kodak color negative film, and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9600 using OpenPrintMaker software on Hahnemühle bamboo paper using Cone Piezography Sepia K7 ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image created in Austin, Texas.

Photographic image created with a Arca-Swiss camera and a Kodak Portrait lens through digital capture with a Better Light Super 6K-HS and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9800 using ColorBurst Raster Image Processing software on Canson Infinity BFK Rives Paper using ConeColor ink.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: J. Scott King is a photographer and printmaker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image created in Quebec, Canada.

Photographic image created with a Leica M6 camera and a Summicron-M 35-millimeter lens on Ilford HP5 Plus film, and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9600 using OpenPrintMaker software on Hahnemühle bamboo paper using Cone Piezography Sepia K7 ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image created in Austin, Texas.

Photographic image created with a Leica M8 camera and a Summicron-M 35-millimeter lens through digital capture and printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9800 using ColorBurst Raster Image Processing software on Canson Infinity BFK Rives Paper using ConeColor ink.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mark Savoia is a photographer and printmaker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic image created on 35-millimeter film and scanned using a Howtek drum scanner printed by an Epson Stylus Pro 9800 using Studio Print Version 12 software on Hahnemühle Photo Gloss Baryta 320 grams per square meter paper using Cone PiezoTone Carbon Sepia ink and coated with ultraviolet varnish
